title: Through effective stock selection and trading, I am deeply grateful to the market, as well as to the mentors and friends who have offered me their assistance.
author: Independent_Gur8648
selftext:
I have come here and while I have chosen to share my primary investment portfolio my intention is neither to recommend specific stock tickers nor to chase after the day's most dazzling star stocks. Rather, I am here simply to express my gratitude to those who have helped me along the way, and to share the trading methods and stock-selection strategies I have developed through my own trial and error.

I devote the majority of my time to screening for stocks that are undervalued, possess a relatively small float, or have been temporarily overlooked by the market; I wait patiently for definitive entry signals instead of blindly chasing market fads or trends. One of the core tenets of my stock-selection philosophy is to look for signs of stability amidst the multi stage evolution of a trend for instance, by observing how a stock's price fluctuates around its 5-day/13-day/34-day/55-day moving averages, as well as monitoring changes in trading volume and liquidity.

This constitutes just one link in my overall workflow, yet it effectively helps me steer clear of random, chaotic price spikes, thereby allowing me to focus my energy on high quality stocks that are currently in a consolidation phase quietly building momentum and poised for a breakout.

I have established a discussion group where, during my spare time, I share some of my insights as well as the potential risks and specific entry points identified by my fellow members. The sole purpose of this post is to pay it forward: to help others, just as others once helped me. We are simply a community of mutual learners; there is no paid content involved, nor are we selling anything. It is a space for open discussion; if you are interested in joining, please feel free to send me a private message directly.
